The Jewish Israeli enemy continues to target paramedics in south Lebanon, and then targeting the medics helping the medics ❗️ Ministry of Public Health: A heinous crime against paramedics in Meifdoun, resulting in the martyrdom of three paramedics, the injury of six, and one paramedic still missing... In detail, an ambulance team affiliated with the Health Authority was targeted while attempting to rescue the wounded, leading to the martyrdom of one paramedic while the second remains missing. When a second ambulance from the Health Authority headed to rescue the paramedics, it was targeted, leaving three paramedics injured. In a third rescue attempt, two ambulances from the Al-Risala Association and Nabatieh Ambulance were dispatched, and they were also targeted, resulting in the martyrdom of two paramedics and the injury of three others.

U.S. and Iranian negotiators are moving closer to a potential agreement, with officials saying they are “getting closer” after recent talks and backchannel efforts. Mediators from Pakistan, Egypt and Türkiye are helping bridge gaps before the ceasefire deadline on April 21, though a deal is not guaranteed. Source: Axios

Magyar said he intends to continue the close relations between Hungary and Israel, and even invited Prime Minister Netanyahu…" Péter Magyar, like Orbán, has invited Netanyahu to Hungary—signaling he would not enforce the International Criminal Court warrant against him. Hungary, as an International Criminal Court member, is obligated to arrest Netanyahu if he enters the country.

⚡️ 🇾🇪 🇾🇪 The Revolutionary Movement for the Liberation and Independence of South Yemen has reorganised its leadership in Aden, appointing Lieutenant Colonel Fahmi Nasser Saeed Al‑Suhaybi as head of the movement in the city, with two deputies. The decree was signed by Fadi Hassan Baoum, head of the political bureau. The move comes as the rival Southern Transitional Council remains weakened after a failed attempt to seize control of south‑eastern Yemen in January. Baoum is positioning his movement to fill the power vacuum and assert leadership in any future independence negotiations. @wfwitness

Pakistan’s Army Chief Asim Munir is heading to Iran with a senior delegation to deliver a U.S. message and coordinate a possible second round of Iran–U.S. talks, potentially to be held soon in Islamabad. Source: Tasnim

Al Mayadeen reports, citing an Iranian source, that a ceasefire in Lebanon may begin tonight under pressure from Iran and last about a week. A senior Israeli official denies this, saying no ceasefire decision has been made. Israeli cabinet will convene tonight. Source: N12

Netanyahu’s Office: Netanyahu and Hungary’s Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar held a warm introductory conversation a short while ago. Magyar said he intends to continue the close relations between Hungary and Israel, and even invited Prime Minister Netanyahu to attend a ceremony marking 70 years since the Hungarian Uprising. Netanyahu welcomed his remarks, responded positively, and invited Prime Minister-elect Magyar to a government-to-government meeting in Jerusalem. Netanyahu expressed confidence that the warm relations that existed with the outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orbán will continue during Prime Minister-elect Magyar’s term. The two agreed that the foreign ministers of their countries will meet soon to discuss the continuation of the close relations between Israel and Hungary.
